name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram operates a network of 16+ locations across San Francisco offering money transfers, bill payments, and cash services through partner retailers like CVS, Ace Cash Express, and local businesses.
description_long: MoneyGram is a global money transfer and financial services company that provides domestic and international money movement solutions. The company operates through a distributed network model, partnering with retail locations, convenience stores, supermarkets, and dedicated money transfer agents rather than maintaining company-owned storefronts. This allows MoneyGram to offer widespread accessibility for consumers needing quick cash transactions. In San Francisco specifically, MoneyGram maintains 16 searchable locations across the city, with partner retailers ranging from major chains (CVS, Lucky supermarket) to specialized money transfer agents (U-Send Express, Cambialo Y Mandalo). The service is available both online and offline, enabling customers to initiate transfers remotely or conduct in-person transactions. MoneyGram's primary value proposition centers on convenience and speed—customers can send and receive money, pay bills, and access cash services at numerous neighborhood locations with extended and early operating hours (many opening at 7:00-8:00 AM). The distributed partner network model distinguishes MoneyGram from traditional banks, as it eliminates the need for dedicated brick-and-mortar locations while leveraging existing retail traffic. This approach has made money transfer services accessible in underserved communities, including immigrant neighborhoods where remittances are critical. However, consumers should note that MoneyGram's fees for transfers and services are not transparent on the location-finder page itself, and actual costs vary significantly based on transfer type, destination, and amount. The reliance on third-party partner locations also means service quality and staff knowledge can vary substantially by agent. For consumers seeking quick, accessible money movement without a traditional bank account, MoneyGram provides practical solutions, but fee structures warrant careful comparison against digital alternatives.
pros: 16+ convenient locations across San Francisco minimizing travel distance for customers
Partnerships with major retailers (CVS, Lucky supermarket) and dedicated agents providing multiple touchpoints
Extended operating hours with many locations opening at 7:00-7:30 AM to serve early commuters
Online and offline service options allowing customers to initiate transfers remotely
Serves underbanked and immigrant communities with accessible money transfer and remittance services
Real-time location search with map integration and phone numbers for immediate access
Quick transaction processing enabling same-day money movement
cons: No fee transparency on website—customers cannot compare costs before visiting a location
Service quality and staff expertise varies significantly across partner retail locations
Limited integration with digital banking—primarily cash and in-person focused, not competitive with fintech alternatives
Reliance on third-party retailers means reduced control over brand experience and customer service consistency
No information provided about transfer speed guarantees, error resolution, or customer support accessibility
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional banking services
Immigrants and workers sending remittances to family members domestically or internationally
Customers needing same-day cash access in specific neighborhoods without reliable bank branches
Small business owners and gig workers requiring quick bill payment and cash movement solutions
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked consumers, immigrants sending remittances, and workers needing rapid cash access in underserved neighborhoods. The primary caveat is that fees and transfer speeds are not disclosed on their website, and service quality depends heavily on individual partner location quality and staff competence.
services: In-person cash pickups at partner locations
Online transfer initiation with offline completion
Cash access points across distributed retail network
Location finder and mapping for nearest agents
